title: Guard (where) — grammar, parser
analysis:
Thorough codebase exploration reveals the task is partially complete. The tree-sitter grammar already defines both where_clause (per-declaration postfix guard, line 56) and guarded_block (block-level guard with optional else, line 62) rules in grammar.js. The generated parser.c (18 references to guarded_block), grammar.json, and node-types.json are all up to date. Per-declaration where clauses are fully implemented: WhereClause struct exists in lib.rs, all 6 declaration types have where_clause: Option<WhereClause> fields, and the lowering in ts_parser.rs correctly extracts where clauses via lower_where_clause (lines 197-211). Two guard tests already exist and pass: param_with_where_clause and bracket_source_no_guards.

What remains is the block-level guarded group feature: (1) AST: Add GuardedGroupDecl struct and MemberDecl::GuardedGroup variant to lib.rs. (2) Lowering: Add lower_guarded_block method and handle guarded_block nodes in lower_structure. (3) Fix exhaustive match arms in ts_parser.rs internal tests (4 locations) and boundary1_producer.rs (1 location). (4) Tests: Block guard, guard with else, nested guards, complex guard expressions, additional per-declaration tests.

The compiler (reify-compiler/src/lib.rs line 807) already has a forward-looking MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(_) => {} stub. The LSP uses wildcard matches. No grammar, parser.c, fixtures.rs, or compiler changes needed.

Key challenge: The guarded_block grammar uses two positional repeat($._member) groups without field names. Lowering must track position relative to the anonymous else keyword to separate main vs else members. The member dispatch logic should be refactored into a reusable lower_member_node helper for recursive guarded block nesting.

prerequisites.description: Add GuardedGroupDecl struct to lib.rs (after WhereClause at line ~46) with fields: condition: Expr, members: Vec<MemberDecl>, else_members: Vec<MemberDecl>, span: SourceSpan, content_hash: ContentHash. Add #[derive(Debug, Clone)]. Add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(GuardedGroupDecl) variant to the MemberDecl enum (after Maximize at line ~38). Required before any new tests can compile.

Fix exhaustive match arms broken by new GuardedGroup variant. Five locations in crates/reify-syntax: (1) ts_parser.rs ts_parse_produces_correct_structure test ~line 600: add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(_) => guarded.into(). (2) ts_parser.rs spans_are_valid test ~line 762: add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g) => g.span. (3) ts_parser.rs content_hashes test ~line 829: add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g) => (g.span, g.content_hash). (4) ts_parser.rs parse_deterministic test ~lines 910+918: add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g) => (g.content_hash, g.span) to both arms. (5) boundary1_producer.rs ~line 353: add MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g) => g.span.

steps.description: Write failing test parse_basic_guarded_block in guard_tests.rs: parse 'structure S { where needs_cooling { param fan_size : Scalar = 50mm } }'. Assert: no parse errors, members.len()==1, members[0] matches MemberDecl::GuardedGroup(g) with g.condition being ExprKind::Ident(needs_cooling), g.members.len()==1 (ParamDecl fan_size), g.else_members.is_empty(). Fails because lower_structure doesn't handle guarded_block nodes yet.

Implement guarded block lowering in ts_parser.rs. (1) Extract member dispatch from lower_structure match block (lines 113-183) into lower_member_node(&mut self, child: Node) -> Option<MemberDecl> handling param/let/constraint/sub/minimize/maximize/guarded_block/ERROR with is_error checks. (2) Refactor lower_structure to call lower_member_node. (3) Add lower_guarded_block(&mut self, node: Node) -> Option<GuardedGroupDecl>: extract condition via child_by_field_name(condition) + lower_expr, iterate children with boolean in_else flag toggled on anonymous child with text else, collect members via lower_member_node into members or else_members, construct GuardedGroupDecl with span and content_hash.

Write test parse_guarded_block_with_else in guard_tests.rs: parse 'structure S { where cond { param a : Scalar = 1mm } else { param b : Scalar = 2mm } }'. Assert: condition is Ident(cond), members has 1 ParamDecl a, else_members has 1 ParamDecl b.

If else test fails, debug lower_guarded_block else delimiter detection. Verify tree-sitter CST structure by inspecting child node kinds/text. The else keyword should appear as anonymous node. Adjust detection logic if needed (check node kind vs text comparison).

Write test parse_nested_guarded_blocks in guard_tests.rs: parse 'structure S { where a { where b { param x : Scalar } } }'. Assert: outer GuardedGroupDecl has condition Ident(a), members[0] is GuardedGroup with condition Ident(b), inner members[0] is ParamDecl x.
Verify nested guards pass. Should already work via recursive lower_member_node dispatch. If failing, check guarded_block case in lower_member_node correctly invokes lower_guarded_block.

Write test parse_guard_complex_expression in guard_tests.rs: (1) Per-declaration: 'param fan : Scalar = 50mm where x > 5mm && active', assert condition is BinOp(&&, BinOp(>, Ident(x), QuantityLiteral(5,mm)), Ident(active)). (2) Block-level: 'where needs_cooling || forced { param fan : Scalar = 50mm }', assert condition is BinOp(||, Ident(needs_cooling), Ident(forced)).
Verify complex guard expressions pass. Should work since condition uses $._expression supporting all operators. If failing, investigate expression boundary parsing.

Write additional per-declaration where tests in guard_tests.rs: (1) let_with_where_clause: 'let y = x * 2 where active' -> LetDecl.where_clause Ident(active). (2) constraint_with_where_clause: 'constraint x > 5mm where enabled' -> ConstraintDecl.where_clause Ident(enabled). (3) sub_with_where_clause: 'sub hole = Hole(diameter: 6mm) where needs_hole' -> SubDecl.where_clause Ident(needs_hole). Should pass immediately.
Verify per-declaration where tests pass. These exercise existing lowering (lower_where_clause called by lower_let, lower_constraint, lower_sub). If any fail, investigate.

Write test guard_free_declarations_backward_compat in guard_tests.rs: parse bracket_source(), verify no GuardedGroup variants in members, all where_clause fields are None, member count unchanged at 10, no errors.
Run cargo test -p reify-syntax then cargo test --workspace. Fix any remaining issues. Compiler with GuardedGroup stub and LSP with wildcards should pass cleanly.

design_decisions.decision: Grammar and generated parser are already complete - no grammar changes needed
design_decisions.rationale: The tree-sitter grammar already defines both where_clause (line 56) and guarded_block (line 62) rules. parser.c was regenerated in prior commit 66866d6. The grammar handles ambiguity correctly: where expr followed by { parses as guarded_block; at end of declaration parses as where_clause.

design_decisions.decision: Refactor member dispatch into lower_member_node helper for reuse
design_decisions.rationale: lower_structure has a monolithic match block for all member kinds. Guarded blocks need identical dispatch for nested members including recursive guarded blocks. Extracting lower_member_node eliminates duplication and enables clean recursion.

design_decisions.decision: Use positional iteration with else delimiter detection for main vs else member separation
design_decisions.rationale: The guarded_block grammar uses two unnamed repeat($._member) groups without field names. Tree-sitter places all member children as direct children. The else keyword appears as anonymous child between groups. Iterate children toggling a boolean on else to partition into main and else vectors.
All 6 existing declaration types carry content_hash for incremental evaluation. GuardedGroupDecl follows the same pattern for consistency.
Grammar is complete. Compiler has forward-looking GuardedGroup stub (line 807). LSP uses wildcards. Only reify-syntax needs changes: AST, lowering, tests.

design_decisions.decision: Per-declaration where clauses already support all 6 member types
design_decisions.rationale: Existing implementation added where_clause to all 6 types (not just the 4 in task spec). Already done and tested. Plan preserves this.

reuse.how: Already implemented for all 6 lowering functions. No changes needed. Guarded block uses direct child_by_field_name(condition) since it has its own condition field.
Reuse for guarded_block condition. Supports all expression types needed for guard conditions.
Extract into lower_member_node. Add guarded_block case. Reuse for both structure body and nested guarded block members.
Reuse for GuardedGroupDecl construction, same pattern as all existing AST nodes.
Reuse for all new guard tests to parse source and extract members.
Follow: parse source -> extract members -> match MemberDecl variant -> assert fields.
